examples.title: Two-Factor Verification
examples.code:
<!-- OTP Two-Factor card with countdown, backup code link, and trust badges -->
<Stack Class="max-w-md mx-auto" Gap="0">
    <Card Class="border-border/40">
        <CardHeader>
            <Flex Align="center" Gap="3">
                <Flex Class="h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] bg-primary/10">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="ShieldCheck" Class="h-5 w-5 text-primary" />
                </Flex>
                <Stack>
                    <Heading Level="2">Two-factor auth</Heading>
                    <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Code sent to j••••@acme.com</Lumeo.Text>
                </Stack>
            </Flex>
        </CardHeader>
        <CardContent>
            <Stack Gap="6">
                <Flex Class="rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] bg-muted/50 border border-border/40 px-4 py-3" Gap="3">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="Info" Class="h-4 w-4 text-muted-foreground" />
                    <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Enter the 6-digit code. Expires in 5:00.</Lumeo.Text>
                </Flex>

                <Flex Justify="center">
                    <OtpInput Length="6" @bind-Value="_otp" OnComplete="HandleOtpComplete" />
                </Flex>

                <Button Class="w-full" IsLoading="_otpLoading" OnClick="HandleOtpVerify">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="ShieldCheck" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Verify code
                </Button>

                <Flex Justify="between">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" OnClick="GoBack">Back</Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" OnClick="ResendCode">Resend code</Button>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
        </CardContent>
    </Card>
</Stack>

@code {
    private string _otp = "";
    private bool _otpLoading;

    private async Task HandleOtpComplete(string otp) { if (otp.Length == 6) await HandleOtpVerify(); }
    private async Task HandleOtpVerify()
    {
        _otpLoading = true;
        await Task.Delay(1400);
        _otpLoading = false;
        ToastService.Success("Verified", "Welcome back!");
    }
    private void ResendCode() { _otp = ""; ToastService.Info("Resent", "New code sent."); }
    private void GoBack() { _otp = ""; }
}

patterns.title: Chat
patterns.description: A full messaging interface with contact list, conversation view, time-grouped messages, and message input bar.

examples.title: Chat Messaging Interface
examples.code:
<!-- Chat layout: contact sidebar | message thread -->
<Stack Class="w-full border border-border/40 rounded-xl overflow-hidden" style="height: 600px;">
    <Flex Class="h-full">

        <!-- Contact Sidebar -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="w-72 border-r border-border/40 bg-card">
            <Flex Class="items-center justify-between px-4 pt-4 pb-3 border-b border-border/40">
                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-base font-bold text-foreground">Messages</Lumeo.Text>
                <Flex Class="items-center gap-1">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Search" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="SquarePen" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                </Flex>
            </Flex>
            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto">
                @foreach (var contact in _contacts)
                {
                    <button class="flex items-start gap-3 w-full px-3 py-2.5 hover:bg-muted/40 transition-colors" @onclick="@(() => SelectContact(contact))">
                        <Stack Class="relative shrink-0">
                            <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm">
                                <AvatarFallback>@contact.Initials</AvatarFallback>
                            </Avatar>
                            @if (contact.IsOnline)
                            {
                                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="absolute bottom-0 right-0 h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full bg-primary border-2 border-card block"></Lumeo.Text>
                            }
                        </Stack>
                        <Stack Class="flex-1 min-w-0 gap-0.5">
                            <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground truncate">@contact.Name</Lumeo.Text>
                                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">@contact.Timestamp</Lumeo.Text>
                            </Flex>
                            <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground truncate">@contact.LastMessage</Lumeo.Text>
                                @if (contact.UnreadCount > 0) { <Badge Class="ml-1 shrink-0 h-4 min-w-4 text-[10px] px-1">@contact.UnreadCount</Badge> }
                            </Flex>
                        </Stack>
                    </button>
                }
            </Stack>
        </Flex>

        <!-- Chat Area -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="flex-1 overflow-hidden bg-background">
            <!-- Header -->
            <Flex Class="items-center gap-3 px-4 py-3 border-b border-border/40 bg-card">
                <Stack Class="relative">
                    <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm"><AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="absolute bottom-0 right-0 h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full bg-primary border-2 border-card block"></Lumeo.Text>
                </Stack>
                <Stack>
                    <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Priya Kapoor</Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-primary">Active now</Lumeo.Text>
                </Stack>
                <Flex Class="ml-auto items-center gap-1">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Phone" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Video" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Ellipsis" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                </Flex>
            </Flex>

            <!-- Messages -->
            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-4 py-4 gap-1">
                <!-- Time group separator -->
                <Flex Class="items-center gap-3 my-3">
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="flex-1 h-px bg-border/40 block"></Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">Today</Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="flex-1 h-px bg-border/40 block"></Lumeo.Text>
                </Flex>
                @foreach (var msg in _messages)
                {
                    <Flex Class="@(msg.IsSent ? \"items-end gap-2 justify-end\" : \"items-end gap-2 justify-start\")">
                        @if (!msg.IsSent)
                        {
                            <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" Class="shrink-0 mb-1"><AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                        }
                        <Stack Class="max-w-sm gap-1">
                            <Lumeo.Text As="div" Class="@(msg.IsSent ? \"rounded-2xl rounded-br-sm px-3.5 py-2.5 text-sm bg-primary text-primary-foreground\" : \"rounded-2xl rounded-bl-sm px-3.5 py-2.5 text-sm bg-muted text-foreground\")">@msg.Text</Lumeo.Text>
                            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground px-1">@msg.Time</Lumeo.Text>
                        </Stack>
                        @if (msg.IsSent)
                        {
                            <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" Class="shrink-0 mb-1"><AvatarFallback>ME</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                        }
                    </Flex>
                }
            </Stack>

            <!-- Input -->
            <Stack Class="border-t border-border/40 bg-card px-4 py-3 gap-2">
                <Flex Class="items-center gap-2 rounded-xl border border-border/40 bg-background px-3 py-2">
                    <Input placeholder="Type a message..." @bind-Value="_newMessage" Class="flex-1 border-0 shadow-none bg-transparent text-sm p-0 h-auto focus-visible:ring-0" />
                </Flex>
                <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                    <Flex Class="items-center gap-1">
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Paperclip" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Smile" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Mic" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                    </Flex>
                    <Button Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm" @onclick="SendMessage">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Send" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1.5" /> Send
                    </Button>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
        </Flex>
    </Flex>
</Stack>

@code {
    private void SendMessage()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_newMessage)) return;
        _messages.Add(new Message(_newMessage, true, DateTime.Now.ToString("h:mm tt")));
        _newMessage = string.Empty;
    }
}

<!-- Top Bar -->
<Flex Class="items-center justify-between gap-4 rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] bg-card px-4 py-3 shadow-sm">

    </Flex>
    <Flex Class="flex-1 max-w-sm items-center gap-2 rounded-md border border-border/40 bg-background px-3 h-9">
        <DynamicIcon Name="Search" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
        <Input Placeholder="Search..." Class="h-8 flex-1 bg-transparent border-0 shadow-none" />
    </Flex>
    <Flex Class="items-center gap-1.5">
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
            <DynamicIcon Name="Bell" Class="h-4 w-4" />
        </Button>
        <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm">
            <AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback>
        </Avatar>
        <DynamicIcon Name="ChevronDown" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
    </Flex>
</Flex>

<!-- Stats Row -->
<Grid Class="grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4">
    @foreach (var stat in _stats)
    {
        <Card Class="rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] border-border/40">
            <CardHeader Class="pb-2">
                <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                    <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wider">@stat.Label</Lumeo.Text>
                    <DynamicIcon Name="@stat.IconName" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />

            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent Class="pt-0">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-2xl font-bold text-foreground">@stat.Value</Lumeo.Text>
                <Flex Class="mt-1.5 items-center gap-1.5">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="@(stat.IsPositive ? "TrendingUp" : "TrendingDown")" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs font-semibold">@stat.Trend</Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">vs last month</Lumeo.Text>
                </Flex>
                <Progress Value="@stat.ProgressValue" Class="mt-3 h-1" Variant="@stat.ProgressVariant" />
            </CardContent>
        </Card>
    }
</Grid>

<!-- Revenue Chart + Activity -->
<Grid Class="grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-7 gap-4">
    <Card Class="lg:col-span-5">
        <CardHeader>
            <Heading Level="3">Revenue Overview</Heading>

        </CardHeader>

</Grid>

patterns.title: Multi-Step Form Wizard
patterns.description: A polished four-step onboarding wizard with progress steps, real field inputs, preferences, and a review summary before submission.

examples.title: Onboarding Wizard
examples.code:
<!-- 4-step onboarding wizard with Steps progress bar, side illustration, and review summary -->
<Stack Class="max-w-3xl mx-auto" Gap="4">

    <!-- Steps progress indicator -->
    <Card>
        <CardContent Class="py-4">
            <Steps CurrentStep="@(_currentStep - 1)" Class="w-full">
                <StepsItem Title="Account" Description="Credentials" />
                <StepsItem Title="Profile" Description="About you" />
                <StepsItem Title="Preferences" Description="Customize" />
                <StepsItem Title="Review" Description="Confirm" />
            </Steps>
        </CardContent>
    </Card>

    <Flex Gap="4" Class="lg:flex-row">
        <!-- Form Card -->
        <Card Class="flex-1">
            <CardHeader>
                <Flex Align="center" Gap="3">
                    <Flex Class="h-9 w-9 items-center justify-center rounded-[var(--radius)] bg-primary/10">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="UserPlus" Class="h-4 w-4 text-primary" />
                    </Flex>
                    <Stack>
                        <Heading Level="3">Create your account</Heading>
                        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Set up your login credentials</Lumeo.Text>
                    </Stack>
                </Flex>
            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent>
                @if (_currentStep == 1)
                {
                    <!-- Step 1: First/Last name, Email, Password + PasswordInput with ShowStrength -->
                }
                @if (_currentStep == 2)
                {
                    <!-- Step 2: Job title, Department Select, Company, Country, Timezone, Bio Textarea -->
                }
                @if (_currentStep == 3)
                {
                    <!-- Step 3: Notification switches, Use-case RadioGroup in styled cards, Interest checkboxes -->
                }
                @if (_currentStep == 4)
                {
                    <!-- Step 4: Review sections with Edit links, Terms checkbox, Submit -->
                }
            </CardContent>
            <CardFooter>
                <Flex Justify="between" Class="w-full">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" OnClick="PreviousStep" Disabled="@(_currentStep == 1)">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="ChevronLeft" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Back
                    </Button>
                    @if (_currentStep < 4)
                    {
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" OnClick="NextStep">
                            Continue <DynamicIcon Name="ChevronRight" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" OnClick="Submit" Disabled="@(!_confirmed)">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Check" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Create Account
                        </Button>
                    }
                </Flex>
            </CardFooter>
        </Card>

        <!-- Side illustration / tips panel -->
        <Card Class="w-64 bg-muted/30 border-border/40">
            <CardContent Class="py-6">
                <!-- Step-specific icon, title, body copy, and tip bullets -->
            </CardContent>
        </Card>
    </Flex>
</Stack>

@code {
    private int _currentStep = 1;
    private bool _confirmed = false;

    private void NextStep() { if (_currentStep < 4) _currentStep++; }
    private void PreviousStep() { if (_currentStep > 1) _currentStep--; }
    private void Submit() { if (_confirmed) { /* create account */ } }
}

            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto">
                @foreach (var mail in _mailItems)

                            </Flex>
                            <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground shrink-0">@mail.Time</Lumeo.Text>
                        </Flex>
                        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground pl-4 truncate">@mail.Subject</Lumeo.Text>
                        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground pl-4 truncate">@mail.Preview</Lumeo.Text>
                    </Stack>
                }
            </Stack>
        </Flex>

        <!-- Reading Pane -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="flex-1 bg-background">
            <Stack Class="p-4 border-b border-border/40 bg-card">
                <Flex Class="items-start justify-between gap-4">
                    <Flex Class="items-start gap-3">
                        <Avatar><AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                        <Stack>
                            <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Priya Kapoor</Lumeo.Text>
                            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-base font-semibold text-foreground mt-1">Re: Q2 roadmap review</Lumeo.Text>
                            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-0.5">Apr 17, 2026, 9:42 AM</Lumeo.Text>
                        </Stack>
                    </Flex>
                    <Flex Class="items-center gap-1">
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Archive" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Trash2" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                    </Flex>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-5">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm text-foreground leading-relaxed">Email body content...</Lumeo.Text>
            </Stack>
            <Separator />
            <Stack Gap="2" Class="p-3 bg-card">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground">Reply to Priya Kapoor</Lumeo.Text>
                <Textarea placeholder="Write your reply..." Class="min-h-[70px] text-sm resize-none" />
                <Flex Class="justify-end gap-2">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Forward" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1.5" /> Forward
                    </Button>
                    <Button Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Send" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1.5" /> Send
                    </Button>

            </Stack>
        </Flex>

</Stack>

        <AlertDialogHeader>

        </AlertDialogHeader>

@code {
    private bool _loadingDialogOpen;
    private bool _isDeleting;

        await Task.Delay(2000);
        _isDeleting = false;
        _loadingDialogOpen = false;
    }
}

parameters.name: ZIndex

AnimatedBeam.name: AnimatedBeam
AnimatedBeam.description: SVG beam that traces an animated gradient path between two DOM elements.

parameters.name: ContainerId
parameters.description: The id of the container element that holds both the from/to nodes and this AnimatedBeam. Coordinates are measured relative to this element (matching Magic UI's containerRef pattern). If null, the SVG's own bounding rect is used as fallback.

parameters.name: FromId
parameters.description: The element id of the source node.

parameters.name: ToId
parameters.description: The element id of the destination node.

parameters.name: DurationMs
parameters.description: Duration of one full beam cycle in milliseconds.

parameters.name: DelayMs
parameters.description: Delay before the first animation cycle starts.

parameters.name: Curvature
parameters.description: Curvature of the bezier path. 0 = straight. Positive = bows up.

parameters.name: Reverse
parameters.description: Reverses the direction of travel.

parameters.name: GradientStartColor
parameters.description: Color at the start of the gradient.

parameters.name: GradientStopColor
parameters.description: Color at the end of the gradient.

parameters.name: PathColor
parameters.description: CSS color of the guide track path.

parameters.name: PathWidth
parameters.description: Width of the guide track in px.

parameters.name: PathOpacity
parameters.description: Opacity of the guide track (0–1).

parameters.name: BeamWidth
parameters.description: Width of the animated beam stroke in px.

BlurFade.name: BlurFade
BlurFade.description: Motion primitive: blur + fade-in on mount or when in view.

parameters.name: ForceHidden
parameters.description: When true, always start in the hidden state and animate in — even if the element is already in the viewport at mount. Default (false) is prerender-friendly: above-the-fold BlurFades stay statically visible to avoid a flash of blurred content on first paint. Set to true for docs demos or controlled "Replay" scenarios where you always want the animation to play.

parameters.name: Href
parameters.description: Optional destination URL. When set, renders as an anchor; otherwise a button.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible label. Required because FABs are usually icon-only. Defaults to "Primary action".

parameters.name: Href
parameters.description: Destination URL. When set, item renders as an <a>; otherwise as a <button>.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Text label shown under the icon. Also used as the accessible name.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Icon slot (use Icon or Blazicon). The Content suffix avoids colliding with the <Icon> component.

parameters.name: Badge
parameters.description: Optional badge (count, dot, or any content) shown on the icon. Int, string, or RenderFragment.

parameters.name: IconSize
parameters.description: Size class applied to the icon container. Defaults to h-5 w-5 to match the original visual. Override to fit larger content like avatars (e.g. "h-7 w-7").

parameters.name: IsActive
parameters.description: Manual active-state override. When null, active is inferred from the current route matching Href.

parameters.name: OnClick
parameters.description: Click handler — fires for both <a> and <button> renderings (link navigation still proceeds).

parameters.name: PressEffect
parameters.description: Visual feedback when the item is pressed. Default is None. Scale is recommended for a tactile feel on mobile.

events.name: OnClick
events.description: Click handler — fires for both <a> and <button> renderings (link navigation still proceeds).

Button.name: Button
Button.description: Versatile button with variants, sizes, icons, and loading states.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: HTML button type. Defaults to Button — explicitly NOT submit. The HTML spec says <button> inside <form> defaults to type="submit", which would submit the form on every click. Lumeo defaults to a regular button so Buttons don't silently submit EditForm; opt in via Type="Submit".

parameters.name: StopPropagation
parameters.description: Stop click propagation to ancestors. Use this instead of the Razor directive @@onclick:stopPropagation on a Lumeo component — that directive creates an onclick parameter that case-insensitively collides with the OnClick parameter, producing a compile error. The directive only works natively on HTML elements; this parameter is the equivalent for the <button> element underneath.

parameters.name: PreventDefault
parameters.description: Prevent the default click action on the underlying <button> element. Same rationale as StopPropagation — Razor's @@onclick:preventDefault directive collides case-insensitively with the OnClick parameter on components.

parameters.name: PressEffect
parameters.description: Visual feedback when the button is pressed down. Default is None (no press animation — original behaviour).

enums.name: ButtonPressEffect
enums.description: Visual press feedback style for Button.

Calendar.name: Calendar
Calendar.description: Date picker calendar grid with single, range, and multi-select modes.

parameters.name: DateTooltip
parameters.description: Optional per-date tooltip text. Returned string becomes the day cell's title attribute (native browser tooltip on hover). Return null or empty for no tooltip on that date. Useful for booking calendars: "Booked by John, 14:00–17:00" or "Sold out".

parameters.name: DateBadge
parameters.description: Optional per-date indicator slot. Returns a RenderFragment rendered absolutely positioned in the bottom-right of the day cell — good for status dots, count badges, icons. Return null for no badge on that date. The slot is non-interactive (pointer-events-none) so clicks still hit the day button.

parameters.name: FirstDayOfWeek
parameters.description: The day of the week to render in the leftmost column. When null (default), uses CultureInfo.CurrentCulture.DateTimeFormat.FirstDayOfWeek — so de-DE / fr-FR / etc. start on Monday automatically, en-US on Sunday. Pass an explicit DayOfWeek to override.

parameters.name: SwipeEnabled
parameters.description: When true (default), users can swipe left/right on the day grid to navigate to the next or previous month on touch devices. Vertical page scroll is unaffected (touch-action: pan-y). Set to false to disable swipe navigation.

Chart.name: Chart
Chart.description: Declarative chart wrapper over ECharts — 30+ types supported.

parameters.name: IsLoading
parameters.description: When true, forces the loading state on top of the chart (consumer is refetching data). Independent of the JS-driven ECharts loading indicator controlled by ShowLoading.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingSkeleton
parameters.description: When true (default), renders the loading state while the chart has not yet rendered for the first time, and whenever IsLoading is true. Set to false to opt out entirely.

parameters.name: SkeletonKind
parameters.description: Shape family for the skeleton. Each chart wrapper presets a sensible default (bars for Bar, pie ring for Pie, etc.) — consumers only override when they want a different silhouette.

parameters.name: SkeletonStyle
parameters.description: Phantom (default) renders the real chart with synthesized placeholder data in a muted palette; ECharts animates the option swap to real data. Silhouette falls back to the legacy SVG overlay skeleton.

parameters.name: PhantomCycleMs
parameters.description: Phantom-mode refresh interval in milliseconds — each tick regenerates the placeholder dataset with a new random seed so bars/lines/slices visibly redistribute while loading. Defaults to 1400ms; clamped to ≥200ms.

parameters.name: PhantomShapeOverride
parameters.description: Explicit override for the phantom layout (category count, series count, stacking, area fill). When null (default), the shape is inferred from the real Option so the phantom mirrors the actual chart structure.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingLabel
parameters.description: When true (default), overlays a small top-right pill containing a spinner and LoadingText on top of the active skeleton — a clear, always-on visual cue that the chart is loading. The pill renders independently of SkeletonStyle so phantom and silhouette both get the same hint. Set to false for bare visuals where the skeleton speaks for itself.

parameters.name: LoadingText
parameters.description: Label rendered next to the spinner inside the loading pill. Set to null or empty to hide the text and show only the spinner. When not set, uses the active locale's "Chart.Loading" string (default: "Loading…").

parameters.name: LabelStrategy
parameters.description: Controls how category-axis labels are rendered. Smart (default): show every label; auto-rotate -30° at 11–16 categories, -60° at 17–24, -75° above 24. ShowAll: force every label with no rotation (may overlap on dense datasets). Auto: let ECharts auto-thin labels that would overlap (original ECharts default — hides every second label when crowded).

parameters.name: LabelRotate
parameters.description: Manual override for category-label rotation in degrees (e.g. -30, -45). When set, takes precedence over the rotation implied by LabelStrategy.

Checkbox.name: Checkbox
Checkbox.description: Binary input with indeterminate state and accessible label.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name used when Label is not set (the checkbox renders without a visible label). Required for WCAG compliance when the checkbox sits alone in a cell or alongside text that isn't linked to it via for / htmlFor.

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: When true, the checkmark draws itself in via stroke-dasharray and the box plays a subtle 200ms scale bounce on check. Default is false.

parameters.name: HapticFeedback
parameters.description: When true, triggers a short haptic vibration via navigator.vibrate() each time the checkbox state changes. Silently no-ops on browsers without the Vibration API (e.g. iOS Safari). Default is false.

Combobox.name: Combobox
Combobox.description: Searchable select with filtering, custom values, and grouping.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Popover open state. Bind via @bind-Open when the Combobox lives inside a parent that re-renders heavily (e.g. an EditForm with active validation) — otherwise a parameter cascade can close the popover mid-click. In the bound (controlled) mode the parent owns state and the popover survives re-renders. When unbound, the component still functions but is vulnerable to the cascade-close race; binding is the recommended pattern.

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: Optional data source. When set, ComboboxContent renders options from this list instead of ChildContent.

parameters.name: ItemValue
parameters.description: Extracts the string value for each item. Defaults to ToString().

parameters.name: ItemText
parameters.description: Extracts the display label for each item. Defaults to ItemValue when null.

parameters.name: ItemGroup
parameters.description: Optional group key extractor. When set, options are grouped under sticky headers.

parameters.name: ItemDisabled
parameters.description: Optional per-item disabled predicate.

parameters.name: ItemTemplate
parameters.description: Optional custom render fragment for an item's content. Receives the raw item object.

parameters.name: Virtualize
parameters.description: When true and Items is set, uses Blazor's Virtualize component for the option list.

parameters.name: ItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated pixel height of each option row used by the Virtualize component. Default 36.

records.name: ComboboxContext
records.signature:
ComboboxContext(
        string? Value, HashSet<string>? Values, string SearchText, bool IsOpen, bool Multiple, bool IsSearchLoading, bool Creatable,
        EventCallback<string> OnSelect, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, EventCallback<string> OnSearch,
        EventCallback<string> OnCreateItem,
        string WrapperId, string ContentId, int FocusedIndex, string? FocusedItemId, string? FocusedItemValue,
        EventCallback<int> SetFocusedIndex,
        Action<string, string> RegisterItem, Action<string> UnregisterItem, int ItemCount,
        bool Invalid, bool Required,
        // Data-bound mode params (null when composition mode)
        IEnumerable<object>? Items,
        Func<object, string> ItemValue,
        Func<object, string>? ItemText,
        Func<object, string?>? ItemGroup,
        Func<object, bool>? ItemDisabled,
        RenderFragment<object>? ItemTemplate,
        bool Virtualize,
        float ItemSize)

DataGrid.name: DataGrid
DataGrid.description: Enterprise grid: sort, filter, inline edit, multi-level group (client + server), pin, virtualize, export.

parameters.name: PageSizeOptions
parameters.description: The list of page-size options shown in the rows-per-page selector. Defaults to { 10, 25, 50, 100 }. Set to e.g. { 10, 50, 100, 200, 300 } for larger datasets, or to an empty array to hide the selector entirely.

parameters.name: ShowSearch
parameters.description: Show the global search box in the toolbar. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowColumnChooser
parameters.description: Show the Columns button (visibility/reorder menu). Default true.

parameters.name: ShowExport
parameters.description: Show the Export button and its format dropdown. Default true. Set false to hide export entirely. For fine-grained per-format control see ExportFormats.

parameters.name: ExportFormats
parameters.description: Which export formats appear in the Export dropdown. Default All. Set to e.g. Csv | Excel to hide PDF in WebAssembly where QuestPDF isn't supported. When no flags are set (or ShowExport is false), the Export button is hidden.

parameters.name: VirtualizeThreshold
parameters.description: Item count above which Blazor's <Virtualize> kicks in. Only relevant when ShowPagination is false (or PageSize > this threshold) — paged grids show at most PageSize rows at once and never trigger virtualization. Default 500.

parameters.name: VirtualItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated row height in CSS pixels — used by <Virtualize> to size the scroll spacers. Tune if your rows are taller (dense badges, two-line cells). Default 41px.

parameters.name: VirtualOverscanCount
parameters.description: Extra rows rendered above and below the viewport to hide pop-in during fast scrolling. Default 3.

parameters.name: GroupByFields
parameters.description: Ordered list of fields for multi-level grouping. When set (non-empty) this takes precedence over the single GroupBy parameter. Each entry must be a column Field. The grid renders nested group rows with increasing indentation per level; per-level aggregates are shown on each group header row.

parameters.name: ShowGroupPanel
parameters.description: Shows a slim bar above the grid that lists the current grouping levels as removable chips, plus a dropdown to add a new level from any Groupable column. Default false.

parameters.name: GroupPanelText
parameters.description: Placeholder/hint text shown in the group panel when no fields are grouped.

parameters.name: ChildItemsSelector
parameters.description: When set, the grid renders a hierarchical tree-grid: this selector returns a row's child rows (null/empty = leaf). Each parent row gets an expand chevron in the tree column (see TreeColumnField). Distinct from DetailTemplate, which is a flat expand-for-detail fragment. Tree-grid mode and grouping are mutually exclusive — when both are configured, tree-grid mode wins.

parameters.name: TreeGridDefaultExpanded
parameters.description: Whether tree nodes start expanded. Default false.

parameters.name: TreeColumnField
parameters.description: Field of the column that carries the tree chevron + indentation. Null (default) uses the first visible column.

parameters.name: OnTreeNodeExpand
parameters.description: Raised when a tree node is expanded or collapsed (the item, plus its new expanded state).

parameters.name: Expandable
parameters.description: When true, adds a small Expand icon to the toolbar. Clicking it renders the grid in a fullscreen modal overlay. Default false.

parameters.name: IsExpanded
parameters.description: When true, the grid is currently rendered in fullscreen. Bind this for programmatic control, or leave unbound to let the grid manage its own state via the toolbar button.

parameters.name: FullscreenTitle
parameters.description: Optional title shown in the fullscreen header bar. Defaults to "Data Grid".

parameters.name: Virtualized
parameters.description: Enables Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization.Virtualize{TItem} with an ItemsProvider — rows are fetched from the backend in scroll-driven windows instead of paged. Requires OnRangeRequest. When active, pagination is hidden automatically and the grid renders a single tall scrollable body.

parameters.name: OnRangeRequest
parameters.description: Range-fetch callback used in virtualised server mode. The grid invokes this whenever the viewport scrolls into an unloaded window. The callback must return the requested slice plus the current total row count so the scroll spacers stay accurate.

parameters.name: OnBatchSave
parameters.description: Fired when the user clicks "Save all" while EditMode is Batch. Args carry the buffered modifications + added rows. On a clean callback return (no exception), the grid's pending-changes buffer is cleared automatically.

parameters.name: ShowAddRow
parameters.description: When true and NewItemFactory is set, renders a thin "+ Add row" trigger below the body. Each click creates a TItem via the factory, pushes it into the added-rows buffer, and immediately shows it inline so cells can be filled in. Only meaningful in Batch.

parameters.name: NewItemFactory
parameters.description: Factory used by the "+ Add row" trigger to produce a fresh TItem. Required when ShowAddRow is true. The returned item is stored in the added-rows buffer; the user fills its cells inline and the row is delivered via OnBatchSave.

parameters.name: ColumnVirtualize
parameters.description: Simplified column virtualization: caps the number of horizontally- rendered data columns at MaxVisibleColumns. Pinned columns always make the cut. Default false.

parameters.name: MaxVisibleColumns
parameters.description: Maximum columns rendered when ColumnVirtualize is true. Default 30.

parameters.name: BatchSaveAllText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "Save all" button. Override for localization.

parameters.name: BatchDiscardText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "Discard" button. Override for localization.

parameters.name: BatchAddRowText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "+ Add row" trigger. Override for localization.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: CultureInfo used for cell value formatting (dates/numbers) and exports. When null (default), falls back to CurrentCulture. Useful when your app sets a different culture than the UI locale — e.g. force de-DE formatting in an English UI.

events.name: OnTreeNodeExpand
events.description: Raised when a tree node is expanded or collapsed (the item, plus its new expanded state).

events.name: OnBatchSave
events.description: Fired when the user clicks "Save all" while EditMode is Batch. Args carry the buffered modifications + added rows. On a clean callback return (no exception), the grid's pending-changes buffer is cleared automatically.

DatePicker.name: DatePicker
DatePicker.description: Calendar popover for picking a single date or range.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Picker visual style. Defaults to Calendar.

parameters.name: DateTimeValue
parameters.description: Optional DateTime? binding. When set, takes precedence over Value. Changes fire both ValueChanged (DateOnly?) and DateTimeValueChanged (DateTime? at midnight).

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: Date format string. When null (default), the display falls back to the current culture's ShortDatePattern — so a de-DE user sees 15.03.2026 and an en-US user sees 3/15/2026 automatically.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Overrides CurrentCulture for parsing and formatting within this picker. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization.

parameters.name: IsDateDisabled
parameters.description: Custom predicate to disable specific dates (e.g. booked rooms, unavailable slots). Returns true for dates that should not be selectable.

parameters.name: DateClass
parameters.description: Custom CSS classes per day cell (e.g. status backgrounds for booking calendars).

parameters.name: DateTooltip
parameters.description: Per-date tooltip text rendered as the day cell's title attribute (native browser tooltip).

parameters.name: DateBadge
parameters.description: Per-date indicator slot (status dots, count badges, icons) rendered absolutely positioned in the bottom-right of the day cell.

parameters.name: AllowKeyboardInput
parameters.description: When true (default), the trigger renders as a typeable <input> in the configured Format. On blur / Enter the buffer is parsed via DateOnly.TryParseExact(string, string, IFormatProvider, System.Globalization.DateTimeStyles, out DateOnly) (with a fallback to DateOnly.TryParse(string, IFormatProvider, System.Globalization.DateTimeStyles, out DateOnly)). On success the value commits via ValueChanged; on failure the buffer reverts and OnParseError fires. Set to false to keep the historic button-only trigger (input readonly). Has no effect for Range, Wheel, Inline, or when a custom TriggerContent is supplied.

parameters.name: OnParseError
parameters.description: Fires when keyboard input cannot be parsed against the active Format (or culture short pattern). The argument is the offending raw buffer.

parameters.name: Inline
parameters.description: Optional controlled-mode override for the popover open state. When non-null, the consumer holds the source of truth — the picker no longer mutates its internal state and instead raises OpenChanged. Use this when the picker lives inside a parent that re-renders heavily (e.g. an EditForm with active DataAnnotationsValidator) and the popover would otherwise close mid-click during a parameter cascade. When null (default), the picker falls back to its internal open state and behaves exactly as before.

parameters.name: FirstDayOfWeek
parameters.description: Leftmost column day of week. Null means culture default.

parameters.name: ShowWeekNumbers
parameters.description: When true, renders an ISO week-number column.

parameters.name: OpenChanged
parameters.description: Fires whenever the popover would open or close. Pair with Open via @bind-Open for the controlled-mode pattern described above.

events.name: OnParseError
events.description: Fires when keyboard input cannot be parsed against the active Format (or culture short pattern). The argument is the offending raw buffer.

events.name: OpenChanged
events.description: Fires whenever the popover would open or close. Pair with Open via @bind-Open for the controlled-mode pattern described above.

enums.name: DatePickerVariant
enums.description: Visual style of the picker. Calendar renders the existing month-grid Calendar (default). Wheel renders three iOS-style snap-scrolling columns (day / month / year) — designed for mobile / touch UIs.

records.name: DatePickerPreset

parameters.name: IsDateDisabled
parameters.description: Custom predicate to disable specific dates (e.g. booked rooms). Returns true for dates that should not be selectable.

parameters.description: Custom CSS classes per day cell — forwarded to inner Calendar.

parameters.name: DateTooltip
parameters.description: Per-date tooltip text rendered as the day cell's title.

parameters.name: DateBadge
parameters.description: Per-date indicator slot rendered absolutely positioned in the bottom-right of the day cell.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: Date format. When null (default) the inner DatePicker falls back to the effective culture's ShortDatePattern — so a de-DE user sees 15.03.2026 and an en-US user sees 3/15/2026 automatically.

DateTimePicker.name: DateTimePicker
DateTimePicker.description: Combined date + time picker with timezone awareness.

parameters.name: OffsetValue
parameters.description: Optional DateTimeOffset binding. When the caller binds this, it takes precedence: writes flow back through OffsetValueChanged with the offset that matches TimeZone at the picked wall-clock time (DST-aware). The plain ValueChanged still fires for back-compat. Recommended over Value when working with multi-zone scenarios.

parameters.name: TimeZone
parameters.description: When non-null, all display/edit happens in this zone — the user picks a wall-clock instant in that zone, and the emitted DateTimeOffset carries the matching offset for that instant (DST-aware). When null, the picker behaves exactly as before (local time, no zone indicator).

parameters.name: ShowTimeZoneLabel
parameters.description: When true (default) and TimeZone is set, renders a small "Europe/Berlin (UTC+1)"-style indicator next to the value.

parameters.name: DateFormat
parameters.description: Date portion format. When null (default) falls back to the effective culture's ShortDatePattern.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Overrides CurrentCulture for date/time formatting. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization.

parameters.name: FirstDayOfWeek
parameters.description: Override the locale-derived first day of week shown by the embedded calendar. Forwarded to the inner Calendar; leave null to fall back to CurrentCulture.

parameters.name: OnBeforeClose

FileUpload.name: FileUpload
FileUpload.description: Drag-and-drop file dropzone with progress and validation.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Size of the trigger when Variant="Button". Mirrors ButtonSize so a button-variant FileUpload can match neighbouring buttons in toolbars or compact forms. Has no visual effect on Dropzone or Avatar variants today.

parameters.name: OnUpload
parameters.description: Consumer-supplied upload handler. Called once per file after selection (or after explicit StartUploadAsync if AutoUpload is false). Receives the item, an IProgress{T} for reporting percent 0-100, and a CancellationToken that fires if the user clicks Cancel. Should return the server-assigned URL/id on success, or throw/return null on failure.

parameters.name: AutoUpload
parameters.description: When true (default), uploads begin immediately after files are selected. When false, the user must click the Upload button to start. Requires OnUpload to be set.

parameters.name: MaxConcurrentUploads
parameters.description: Maximum number of OnUpload calls running simultaneously.

parameters.name: ChunkSize
parameters.description: Optional chunk size hint (bytes) for consumers that want to implement chunked/resumable upload inside their OnUpload handler. The component passes this value through ChunkSize but does NOT implement chunking itself.

parameters.name: OnFilesSelected
parameters.description: Fires when files are selected (raw args, kept for back-compat).

parameters.name: OnFileUploaded
parameters.description: Fires each time a file upload succeeds.

parameters.name: OnAllUploaded
parameters.description: Fires when all queued uploads have finished (succeeded, failed, or cancelled).

parameters.name: OnFileFailed
parameters.description: Fires each time a file upload fails.

parameters.name: OnFileRemoved
parameters.description: Fires when a file is removed from the list.

parameters.name: OnFileRejected
parameters.description: Fires when a file is rejected due to type/size/count constraints.

events.name: OnFilesSelected
events.description: Fires when files are selected (raw args, kept for back-compat).

events.name: OnFileUploaded
events.description: Fires each time a file upload succeeds.

events.name: OnAllUploaded
events.description: Fires when all queued uploads have finished (succeeded, failed, or cancelled).

events.name: OnFileFailed
events.description: Fires each time a file upload fails.

events.name: OnFileRemoved
events.description: Fires when a file is removed from the list.

events.name: OnFileRejected
events.description: Fires when a file is rejected due to type/size/count constraints.

examples.title: Upload with progress (fake delay)
examples.code:
<FileUpload
    Multiple="true"
    Label="Upload files"
    OnUpload="@SimulateUpload"
    OnFileUploaded="@(item => _uploadedCount++)"
    OnAllUploaded="@(items => _allDoneMsg = $"All {items.Count} file(s) done!")"  />

@code {
    private async Task<string?> SimulateUpload(
        FileUpload.FileUploadItem item,
        IProgress<int> progress,
        CancellationToken ct)
    {
        for (int pct = 0; pct <= 100; pct += 10)
        {
            ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
            progress.Report(pct);
            await Task.Delay(50, ct);
        }
        return $"https://cdn.example.com/{item.Name}";
    }
}

Map.name: Map
Map.description: Interactive geographic map powered by MapLibre GL — markers, polylines, polygons, circles, arcs, heatmaps, legend overlays, and popups; CARTO vector basemaps, no API key required.

parameters.name: TileLayer
parameters.description: Accepts a CARTO style preset (Positron, DarkMatter, Voyager), a legacy raster name (OpenStreetMap, CartoLight, CartoDark, Satellite, Terrain) — kept as aliases pointing at the closest GL vector style — or a raw MapLibre style.json URL. Auto (the default) picks Positron or DarkMatter based on the host page's theme, matching mapcn.dev.

parameters.name: Layers
parameters.description: When set, renders a chip-style switcher in the top-right that lets the user pick between presets (or custom { name, url } records).

parameters.name: Cluster
parameters.description: Marker clustering via leaflet.markercluster. CDN-loaded on first use; falls back to plain markers if the script fails to fetch.

parameters.name: Geolocate
parameters.description: Adds a "My location" button that geolocates the user.

parameters.name: Fullscreen
parameters.description: Adds a fullscreen toggle button.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.description: Adds a metric scale bar in the bottom-left corner.

parameters.name: Search
parameters.description: Adds a Nominatim-backed search bar in the top-left.

parameters.name: Projection
parameters.description: MapLibre projection name. "mercator" (default flat web-map) or "globe" for a 3-D spherical view. The globe becomes visible at zoom levels roughly 0–3; it blends back to mercator above zoom 4.

parameters.name: AutoFitBounds
parameters.description: When true the map automatically fits its viewport to include all registered markers and shapes after the first render.

parameters.name: Overlay
parameters.description: Optional content rendered as an absolutely-positioned overlay inside the map container (z-index 10, pointer-events none on the wrapper so map interaction is not blocked). Use absolute-position classes on your own child element to place chips, legends, or status badges (e.g. class="absolute bottom-2 right-2 pointer-events-auto …").

records.name: MapContext
records.signature: MapContext(Map Owner)
records.description: Cascading context passed to MapMarker + shape children so they can register themselves with the parent Map.

parameters.name: Curvature
parameters.description: How tall the arc rises above the straight chord, as a fraction of the chord's length. 0 = straight line, 1 = a full semicircle-ish bulge. Defaults to 0.25.

parameters.name: DashArray
parameters.description: SVG dash pattern. Defaults to "5 6" (arcs are dashed by default).

parameters.name: Dashed
parameters.description: Set to false to draw the arc as a solid line instead of the default dashed style. Has no effect when DashArray is set explicitly.

parameters.name: HoverColor
parameters.description: Stroke color on hover. Null = no hover highlight.

parameters.name: HoverWeight
parameters.description: Stroke width on hover. Null = same as Weight.

parameters.name: Animate
parameters.description: Animate the arc drawing on first render (line draws from start to end over ~1 s).

parameters.name: Center
parameters.description: Circle center as (lat, lon).

parameters.name: RadiusMeters
parameters.description: Radius in meters. Leaflet draws the circle in real-world units, so its pixel size grows/shrinks with zoom.

parameters.name: Points
parameters.type: IEnumerable<(double Lat, double Lon, double Intensity)>
parameters.description: Collection of heat points. Each tuple is (lat, lon, intensity) where intensity should be in the range 0–1 (defaults to 1 when not meaningful). Pass a bare (lat, lon) list via a helper or use the convenience constructor that sets intensity to 1.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: Radius of each point in pixels at full zoom. Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: Opacity
parameters.description: Opacity of the heatmap layer (0–1). Defaults to 0.8.

parameters.name: ColorRamp
parameters.description: Color ramp expressed as a MapLibre interpolation stops list. Format: alternating stop (0–1) and CSS color values. Example: new object[] { 0, "transparent", 0.4, "blue", 0.8, "red", 1, "orange" }. Defaults to a blue → cyan → yellow → orange → red ramp.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Optional title shown above the legend items.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.description: Absolute-position corner shorthand. Defaults to "bottom-left". Values: "top-left", "top-right", "bottom-left", "bottom-right". Can also pass a raw Tailwind class via Class.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Dot color. Friendly names ("red", "green", etc.) or any CSS color value.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label text shown next to the dot.

parameters.name: IconUrl
parameters.description: Bitmap icon URL. When set, takes precedence over Variant — we hand Leaflet a plain L.icon and skip the DivIcon path entirely. Anchored bottom-center at 32×32, matching the pre-3.1 default.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual variant for the auto-generated DivIcon. Ignored when IconUrl is set. Defaults to "Default" (filled circle). Valid values: "Default", "Outline", "Pin", "Dot".

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Override the marker's theme color. Accepts a friendly name ("red", "green", "yellow", …) or a raw CSS color ("#ff0000", "hsl(...)"). Null = theme primary.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Permanent label rendered above the marker. Use for waypoints or numbered tours. Different from Title (hover tooltip).

parameters.name: LabelHoverOnly
parameters.description: When true, the Label is hidden by default and only appears on hover. Useful for dense maps where always-on labels create visual noise. Default is false (label always visible).

parameters.name: Icon
parameters.description: Optional inline content inside the marker circle — a single letter, a number, or a small <Blazicon> SVG. The fragment is rendered to a hidden host and its innerHTML is spliced into the DivIcon. Ignored when IconUrl is set.

parameters.name: Popup
parameters.description: Optional rich popup body. When present, it overrides the plain Title as the marker's popup content. The fragment renders into a hidden host div and Leaflet uses the host's innerHTML.

parameters.name: Draggable
parameters.description: Allow the user to drag the marker to a new position.

parameters.name: OnDragEnd
parameters.description: Fires with the new (lat, lon) after the user finishes dragging the marker. Use with two-way binding or to update your own state. Only fires when Draggable is true.

events.name: OnDragEnd
events.description: Fires with the new (lat, lon) after the user finishes dragging the marker. Use with two-way binding or to update your own state. Only fires when Draggable is true.

parameters.name: Points
parameters.description: Ordered list of (lat, lon) points forming the polygon ring. The first and last points are connected automatically.

parameters.name: Points
parameters.description: Ordered list of (lat, lon) points the polyline visits.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Stroke color. Friendly name ("red", "blue", …) or a raw CSS color. Null = theme primary.

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.description: Stroke width in pixels.

parameters.name: Opacity
parameters.description: Stroke opacity, 0..1.

parameters.name: DashArray
parameters.description: SVG dash pattern (e.g. "6 4" for dashed). Null = solid.

parameters.name: Dashed
parameters.description: Convenience toggle — renders a standard "6 4" dash when true. Ignored when DashArray is set explicitly.

parameters.name: HoverColor
parameters.description: Stroke color on hover. Null = no hover highlight.

parameters.name: HoverWeight
parameters.description: Stroke width on hover. Null = same as Weight.

parameters.name: Animate
parameters.description: Animate the line drawing on first render (draws from start to end over ~1 s).

parameters.name: Tooltip
parameters.description: Optional hover tooltip text.

parameters.name: PopupHtml
parameters.description: Optional popup HTML (rendered on click).

parameters.name: Lat
parameters.description: Latitude of the popup anchor point.

parameters.name: Lon
parameters.description: Longitude of the popup anchor point.

parameters.name: IsOpen
parameters.description: Whether the popup is currently visible.

parameters.name: CloseButton
parameters.description: Show a close (×) button inside the popup.

parameters.name: CloseOnClick
parameters.description: Close the popup when the user clicks the map background.

parameters.name: Html
parameters.description: HTML string for the popup body. Overrides ChildContent.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Blazor render fragment for rich popup body. Rendered into a hidden host element; JS reads the innerHTML to open the MapLibre popup.

Pagination.name: Pagination
Pagination.description: Page number bar with prev/next and configurable ranges.

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Pagination>
    <PaginationContent>
        <PaginationPrevious OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage - 1))"
                           Disabled="@(_currentPage <= 1)" />
        @for (int i = 1; i <= _totalPages; i++)
        {
            var page = i;
            <PaginationItem IsActive="@(page == _currentPage)"
                           OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(page))">
                @page
            </PaginationItem>
        }
        <PaginationNext OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage + 1))"
                       Disabled="@(_currentPage >= _totalPages)" />
    </PaginationContent>
</Pagination>

@code {
    private int _currentPage = 1;
    private int _totalPages = 5;

    private void GoToPage(int page)
    {
        if (page >= 1 && page <= _totalPages)
            _currentPage = page;
    }
}

examples.title: Compact
examples.code:

        <PaginationNext OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage + 1))" Disabled="@(_currentPage >= _totalPages)" />
    </PaginationContent>
</Pagination>

QueryBuilder.name: QueryBuilder
QueryBuilder.description: Visual AND/OR predicate-tree builder; serializes to JSON or a LINQ predicate.

parameters.name: Fields
parameters.description: The fields the consumer can build conditions against. Required.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The query tree. Two-way bindable via @bind-Query. When null on first render, an empty root group (And, no rules) is created.

parameters.name: ShowJsonPreview
parameters.description: When true, renders a read-only JSON view of the query below the builder.

parameters.name: MaxDepth
parameters.description: Maximum group nesting depth. The "Add group" button is disabled at this depth.

parameters.name: AllowEmptyGroups
parameters.description: When false, "Remove rule" / "Remove group" are hidden when it would leave a group empty (the root may always be empty).

parameters.name: ValueEditorTemplate
parameters.description: Optional override for how a rule's value editor renders. Receives the rule. When supplied, replaces the built-in per-type editor entirely (the consumer is then responsible for binding to Value and calling NotifyChanged via the cascaded context — see docs).

records.name: QueryBuilderContext
records.signature:
QueryBuilderContext(
        IReadOnlyList<QueryField> Fields,
        int MaxDepth,
        bool AllowEmptyGroups,
        RenderFragment<QueryRule>? ValueEditorTemplate,
        Func<Task> NotifyChanged)
records.description: Cascaded state shared with the recursive QueryBuilderGroup renderer.

parameters.name: OnInitError

@code {
    private bool _open;
    private SchedulerDateRange? _range;
    private string _title = "";
    private List<SchedulerEvent> _events = new();

    void HandleDateSelect(SchedulerDateRange r) { _range = r; _open = true; }
    void Add() {
        _events = _events.Append(new SchedulerEvent(
            Id: Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"),
            Title: _title,
            Start: _range!.Start,

    }
}

Select.name: Select
Select.description: Native-feeling styled dropdown with search and groups.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Popover open state. Bind via @bind-Open when the Select lives inside a parent that re-renders heavily (e.g. an EditForm with active validation) — otherwise a parameter cascade can close the popover mid-click. In the bound (controlled) mode the parent owns state and the popover survives re-renders. When unbound, the component still functions but is vulnerable to the cascade-close race; binding is the recommended pattern.

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: Optional data source. When set, SelectContent renders options from this list instead of ChildContent.

parameters.name: ItemValue
parameters.description: Extracts the string value for each item. Defaults to ToString().

parameters.name: ItemText
parameters.description: Extracts the display label for each item. Defaults to ItemValue when null.

parameters.name: ItemGroup
parameters.description: Optional group key extractor. When set, options are grouped under sticky headers.

parameters.name: ItemDisabled
parameters.description: Optional per-item disabled predicate.

parameters.name: ItemTemplate
parameters.description: Optional custom render fragment for an item's content. Receives the raw item object.

parameters.name: Virtualize
parameters.description: When true and Items is set, uses Blazor's Virtualize component for the option list.

parameters.name: ItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated pixel height of each option row used by the Virtualize component. Default 36.

parameters.name: Animation
parameters.description: How the sheet enters/leaves. Slide (default) animates from the configured Side. Fade uses a simple opacity tween — useful in dense forms where popovers and animations compete. None disables the animation entirely.

parameters.name: SwipeToClose
parameters.description: When true, the sheet can be dismissed by swiping in the direction opposite to its Side (e.g. swipe-down on a Bottom sheet). Default false to avoid conflicts with scrollable content. Wire to true only when the sheet's content fits without scrolling.

parameters.name: ZIndex
parameters.description: Backdrop z-index. Content sits at ZIndex + 1. Defaults to BaseZIndex; OverlayProvider supplies an incrementing value per nested overlay.

Slider.name: Slider
Slider.description: Range slider with single and dual thumb modes.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name exposed via aria-label on the underlying range input. Required for WCAG compliance when the slider is not visually labelled by adjacent text connected via for/htmlFor. For range sliders (IsRange=true) the end handle's aria-label is automatically suffixed with "(end)".

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Culture used for the default tooltip/mark value formatting. When null (default) falls back to CurrentCulture. Ignored when FormatTooltip is supplied.

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Slider @bind-Value="_value" />
<Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Value: @_value</Lumeo.Text>

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: Optional selector that returns a stable, unique key for each item. Providing this ensures Blazor's diffing engine tracks items by identity rather than index — critical for drag-reorder to map DOM state correctly. If omitted, the item reference itself is used as the key.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name for the switch button, exposed via aria-label. Recommended for icon-only switches that have no visible text. If omitted but OnLabel/OffLabel are set, the current one is used automatically so screen readers announce the switch's purpose.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Switch size. Supports Sm, Md (default), Lg.

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: When true, the thumb animates with a bouncy spring cubic-bezier timing instead of the default linear transition. Default is false.

parameters.name: HapticFeedback
parameters.description: When true, triggers a short haptic vibration via navigator.vibrate() each time the switch is toggled. Silently no-ops on browsers without the Vibration API (e.g. iOS Safari). Default is false.

Tabs.name: Tabs
Tabs.description: Tabbed content with keyboard nav and animated active indicator.

parameters.name: AnimatedIndicator
parameters.description: When true, renders a sliding underline that animates between active triggers inside each TabsList. Default is false (no underline, original behaviour).

parameters.name: RenderMode
parameters.description: Controls when each TabsContent panel is rendered into the DOM and whether its state survives a tab switch: Active (default) — only the active panel is in the DOM; switching away unmounts it (form inputs, scroll position and any component state are lost). Lightest footprint. Lazy — a panel is rendered the first time its tab becomes active, then kept in the DOM (hidden) so its state is preserved on subsequent switches. Like DevExpress OnDemand. Eager — every panel is rendered immediately; inactive ones are hidden. State always preserved, but the heaviest initial render. Like DevExpress Always. A single TabsContent can opt into eager rendering regardless of this setting via ForceMount="true".

parameters.name: SwipeEnabled
parameters.description: When true, a horizontal pointer drag (>50 px horizontal, <30 px vertical) on any TabsContent panel switches to the next or previous tab. Default is false (opt-in).

parameters.name: SwipeWrap
parameters.description: When true, swiping past the last tab wraps around to the first (and vice-versa). Default is false (no wrap-around at boundaries).

enums.name: TabsRenderMode
enums.description: Render strategy for TabsContent panels. See RenderMode for the semantics of each value.

parameters.name: ForceMount
parameters.description: When true, this panel is always rendered into the DOM (hidden when inactive), regardless of the parent RenderMode. Use for panels whose state must survive every tab switch — e.g. a half-filled form — while leaving the rest of the tab set on the lighter default mode.

parameters.name: Reorderable
parameters.description: When true, each enabled TabsTrigger becomes draggable (HTML5 drag-and-drop on desktop, touch drag on mobile). On a successful reorder the library raises OnReorder with the from/to indices and moved value — the library does NOT mutate the order. The consumer's handler is responsible for updating the underlying collection and re-rendering.

parameters.name: OnReorder
parameters.description: Fires when the user drops a dragged TabsTrigger on a new position. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true.

parameters.name: SavedLayout
parameters.description: Optional previously-persisted tab ordering. The library does not mutate the consumer's backing collection on its own — pass this to your parent component on init and apply Order to your collection before rendering. Mirrors DataGrid's SavedLayout parameter and pairs with OnLayoutChanged to round-trip the order through your storage of choice.

parameters.name: OnLayoutChanged
parameters.description: Fires immediately after each successful reorder with the post-drop tab order, so the consumer can persist it (e.g. to local storage). The snapshot reflects the order the consumer's collection will have once it applies the corresponding OnReorder. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the order through storage.

events.name: OnReorder
events.description: Fires when the user drops a dragged TabsTrigger on a new position. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true.

events.name: OnLayoutChanged
events.description: Fires immediately after each successful reorder with the post-drop tab order, so the consumer can persist it (e.g. to local storage). The snapshot reflects the order the consumer's collection will have once it applies the corresponding OnReorder. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the order through storage.

Toolbar.name: Toolbar

parameters.name: Overflow
parameters.description: When true the toolbar collapses items that don't fit into a trailing "⋯" dropdown. Use VisibleCount to control how many items are shown inline, or leave it at -1 (the default) to let the toolbar auto-measure via ResizeObserver.

parameters.name: VisibleCount
parameters.description: Number of leading items to show inline when Overflow is true. -1 (default) — auto-measure mode: a ResizeObserver measures the container width on every resize and computes the maximum number of items that fit before a "⋯" trigger button is needed. No consumer configuration required. Positive integer — manual mode: exactly that many items are shown inline and the rest collapse into the "⋯" dropdown regardless of available space.

parameters.name: OverflowLabel
parameters.description: aria-label for the "⋯" overflow button. Default: "More".

enums.name: ToolbarRenderSlot

records.name: ToolbarOverflowContext
records.signature: ToolbarOverflowContext(ToolbarRenderSlot Slot, int VisibleCount, int TotalCount)
records.description: Cascaded by Toolbar to all descendant items when overflow mode is active. Items use this to decide whether to render normally (Visible slot) or as a dropdown row (Dropdown slot), and whether to hide themselves when they fall past the VisibleCount boundary.

records.name: TourStepConfig
records.signature: TourStepConfig(string? TargetSelector, string Title, string? Description = null, Lumeo.Side Placement = Lumeo.Side.Bottom)

examples.title: Basic Tour
examples.code:
<Flex Class="gap-3 flex-wrap">
    <Button id="tour-feature-1" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Dashboard</Button>
    <Button id="tour-feature-2" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Team</Button>
    <Button id="tour-feature-3" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Settings</Button>
</Flex>
<Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" OnClick="@(() => _tourOpen = true)">
    Start Tour
</Button>

<Tour @bind-Open="_tourOpen"
      Steps="@_tourSteps"
      OnComplete="@HandleComplete"
      OnSkip="@(() => _tourOpen = false)" />

@code {
    private bool _tourOpen;

    private List<Tour.TourStepConfig> _tourSteps = new()
    {
        new("#tour-feature-1", "Dashboard", "Get an overview of your key metrics.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#tour-feature-2", "Team Management", "Invite members and manage permissions.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#tour-feature-3", "Settings", "Customize your workspace and account.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
    };

    private void HandleComplete() => _tourOpen = false;
}

examples.title: Custom Placements
examples.code:
<Flex Class="justify-between gap-3">
    <Button id="tour-top"    Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Top</Button>
    <Button id="tour-right"  Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Right</Button>
    <Button id="tour-bottom" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Bottom</Button>
    <Button id="tour-left"   Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Left</Button>
</Flex>
<Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Secondary" OnClick="@(() => _open = true)">
    Tour Placements
</Button>

<Tour @bind-Open="_open"
      Steps="@_steps"
      OnComplete="@(() => _open = false)"
      OnSkip="@(() => _open = false)" />

@code {
    private bool _open;

    private List<Tour.TourStepConfig> _steps = new()
    {
        new("#tour-top",    "Top Placement",    "Tooltip above the element.",  Lumeo.Side.Top),
        new("#tour-right",  "Right Placement",  "Tooltip to the right.",       Lumeo.Side.Right),
        new("#tour-bottom", "Bottom Placement", "Tooltip below the element.",  Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#tour-left",   "Left Placement",   "Tooltip to the left.",        Lumeo.Side.Left),
    };
}
